source_pr.title: session: fix data race in ParseWithParamsInternal
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #30918 

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
fix data race in `ParseWithParamsInternal`
```

symptoms: session: fix data race in ParseWithParamsInternal
failure_signature: session: fix data race in ParseWithParamsInternal
root_cause_explanation: `ParseWithParamsInternal` toggled a shared session variable (`SessionVars.InRestrictedSQL`) around parsing via a save/restore pattern. When parsing happened concurrently, this unsynchronized mutation of shared session state could trigger a data race. This PR fixes the issue by removing the shared-state toggle and instead passing an explicit flag into `ParseWithParams` (and updating call sites), avoiding concurrent mutation of `SessionVars` during parse.
fix_pattern: Avoid mutating shared session variables from concurrent code paths; pass explicit flags/parameters (instead of toggling `SessionVars` state) to make parsing thread-safe.

evidence: evidence: session: fix data race in ParseWithParamsInternal
